title: Salesforce acquires AI customer service platform Fin for $3.6 billion
summary: Salesforce says it wants to use Fin's team and technology to improve Agentforce, its existing enterprise platform that businesses can use to build custom AI agents that automate tasks.
title_es: Salesforce adquiere plataforma IA Fin por $3.6 mil millones
bajada: Salesforce compró Fin, una plataforma de servicio al cliente basada en IA, para fortalecer Agentforce, su plataforma empresarial para construir agentes IA personalizados. Esta adquisición marca una apuesta significativa de Salesforce por consolidar su oferta de automatización empresarial mediante agentes inteligentes.
bajada_en: Salesforce acquired Fin, an AI-powered customer service platform, to strengthen Agentforce, its enterprise platform for building custom AI agents. This acquisition represents a major bet by Salesforce to consolidate its enterprise automation offering through intelligent agents.

title: Cybersecurity vets protest ‘dangerous’ US government ban on Anthropic’s most powerful models
summary: A group made up of dozens of cybersecurity experts urged the White House to remove export control restrictions on Anthropic’s models Fable and Mythos, arguing that the order is going to limit the ability of cybersecurity defenders to secure their software and products.
title_es: Expertos en ciberseguridad protestan restricción a modelos Anthropic
bajada: Docenas de expertos en ciberseguridad pidieron a la Casa Blanca remover restricciones de exportación sobre los modelos Fable y Mythos de Anthropic, argumentando que limitarán la capacidad de defensa cibernética. El debate plantea tensiones entre seguridad nacional y capacidades defensivas empresariales.
bajada_en: Dozens of cybersecurity experts urged the White House to remove export restrictions on Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models, arguing they would limit defensive cybersecurity capabilities. The debate highlights tensions between national security and enterprise defensive capabilities.

title: China may have accessed Mythos
summary: According to a new report from Semafor, the White House's decision to impose export restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos was driven in part by fears that it had been accessed by a group linked to China. If the Chinese government actually had access to Mythos 5 or Fable 5, it would present a serious national security […]
title_es: Reporte: China pudo haber accedido a modelo Mythos de Anthropic
bajada: Según Semafor, la decisión de la Casa Blanca de imponer restricciones de exportación a Mythos fue motivada por temores de que un grupo vinculado a China accediera al modelo. El reporte intensifica preocupaciones sobre seguridad nacional en torno a modelos IA avanzados.
bajada_en: According to Semafor, the White House's decision to impose export restrictions on Mythos was driven by fears that a China-linked group accessed the model. The report intensifies national security concerns around advanced AI models.

title: Introducing the OpenAI Partner Network
summary: OpenAI launches the Partner Network, investing $150M to help global partners accelerate enterprise AI adoption, deployment, and transformation.
title_es: OpenAI lanza Partner Network con inversión de $150M
bajada: OpenAI anunció un programa de partners con $150 millones de inversión destinados a acelerar la adopción, implementación y transformación de IA empresarial global. Esta iniciativa refleja la estrategia de OpenAI de expandir su ecosistema más allá de productos directos.
bajada_en: OpenAI announced a Partner Network program with $150 million in investments to accelerate enterprise AI adoption, deployment, and transformation globally. This initiative reflects OpenAI's strategy to expand its ecosystem beyond direct products.

title: Sarvam becomes India’s newest AI unicorn with $234 million funding round led by HCLTech
summary: Indian IT services company HCLTech is investing $150 million in the Bengaluru startup.
title_es: Sarvam se convierte en unicornio IA de India con $234M
bajada: La startup india Sarvam alcanzó estatus de unicornio con una ronda de financiación de $234 millones liderada por HCLTech, con inversión de $150 millones de la empresa de TI. Este hito refleja el crecimiento acelerado del ecosistema IA en India.
bajada_en: Indian startup Sarvam achieved unicorn status with a $234 million funding round led by HCLTech, which invested $150 million. This milestone reflects accelerated growth in India's AI ecosystem.

title: As AI agents become employees, NewCore emerges with $66M to give them identities
summary: NewCore argues the next challenge in enterprise security will be managing AI agents, not people.
title_es: NewCore recauda $66M para gestionar identidades de agentes IA
bajada: NewCore emergió con $66 millones de financiación argumentando que el próximo desafío en seguridad empresarial será gestionar agentes IA como entidades con identidades propias. La startup aborda un aspecto crítico conforme los agentes IA se integran más en operaciones empresariales.
bajada_en: NewCore emerged with $66 million in funding, arguing that managing AI agents as entities with distinct identities will be the next enterprise security challenge. The startup addresses a critical aspect as AI agents integrate deeper into business operations.

title: This man with ALS is “the first power user” of a brain implant that lets him speak
summary: Casey Harrell has had a set of electrodes embedded in his brain for almost three years. Harrell, who has amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) and is paralyzed, first used his brain-computer interface (BCI) to “speak” sentences with the help of a research team in 2023. Since then, Harrell has clocked thousands of hours of use. He…
title_es: Paciente con ELA controla implante cerebral para hablar
bajada: Casey Harrell, diagnosticado con ELA y paralizado, se ha convertido en el "primer usuario avanzado" de una interfaz cerebro-computadora tras casi tres años de uso acumulando miles de horas. El caso demuestra el potencial transformador de BCIs en restaurar capacidades de comunicación.
bajada_en: Casey Harrell, diagnosed with ALS and paralyzed, has become the "first power user" of a brain-computer interface after almost three years, accumulating thousands of hours of use. The case demonstrates the transformative potential of BCIs in restoring communication abilities.

title: A satellite just learned to find things on its own — here’s what that means
summary: In April, for the first time ever, an Earth observation satellite found what it was looking for, all on its own.
title_es: Satélite aprende a encontrar objetivos por sí solo
bajada: En abril, un satélite de observación terrestre encontró por primera vez lo que buscaba de manera autónoma utilizando IA. Este hito abre posibilidades para monitoreo ambiental, desastres y aplicaciones de seguridad sin intervención humana continua.
bajada_en: In April, an Earth observation satellite found what it was looking for autonomously using AI for the first time. This milestone opens possibilities for autonomous environmental monitoring, disaster response, and security applications.

title: The Coin Flip Judge? Reliability and Bias in LLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation
summary:
arXiv:2606.13685v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: LLM-as-a-Judge is now widely used to rank model outputs, train reward models, and populate public leaderboards, but its run-to-run reliability remains under-characterized. We study repeated identical evaluations on 29 tasks spanning 10 categories using two OpenAI judge models (GPT-4o-mini and GPT-4.1-mini), with 50 pairwise trials and 50 pointwise trials per question, supplemented by temperature and prompt-sensitivity ablations. Across judges, pairwise preferences flip on average 13.6% of the time, with 28% of questions exceeding a 20% flip rate and one question reaching 56%. GPT-4o-mini also exhibits a significant first-position bias (72% A-majority, p = 0.024). At the same time, mean pointwise score gaps are small (0.19--0.36 on a 10-point scale) and not statistically significant in aggregate, producing a pairwise--pointwise gap: judges frequently choose a winner even when their own scalar scores provide little evidence of a meaningful quality difference. Beyond within-judge instability, cross-judge agreement is only 76% ($\kappa = 0.51$), semantically equivalent prompt templates change majority outcomes in 25% of tested cases, and deterministic decoding reduces but does not eliminate inconsistency. A reliability curve analysis shows that, in our dataset, 11 repeated trials are needed for a majority vote to recover the 50-trial reference verdict with 95% probability on average, rising to 15 for high-variance questions. These findings suggest that single-trial LLM judging is often too noisy for high-stakes evaluation, and that multi-trial aggregation, position randomization, and explicit uncertainty reporting should be standard practice. Because both judges are from a single provider, cross-provider replication remains an important next step.
title_es: Estudio: confiabilidad de LLM-as-Judge es problemática
bajada: Investigadores encontraron que usar LLMs como jueces para evaluar modelos es poco confiable: preferencias pairwise cambian 13.6% entre evaluaciones idénticas y acuerdo entre jueces es solo 76%. El hallazgo cuestiona la validez de leaderboards y evaluaciones basadas en LLMs.
bajada_en: Researchers found that using LLMs as judges to evaluate models is unreliable: pairwise preferences flip 13.6% between identical evaluations and cross-judge agreement is only 76%. The finding questions the validity of LLM-based leaderboards and evaluations.

title: We’re strengthening our presence in Alabama through new investments and community support.
summary: Google has announced a $1.5 billion investment for 2026 and 2027 to expand its data center campus in Jackson County, Alabama. Operating since 2019 on a repurposed former…
title_es: Google invierte $1.5 mil millones en centro de datos en Alabama
bajada: Google anunció una inversión de $1.5 mil millones para 2026-2027 en expansión de su campus de centro de datos en Jackson County, Alabama. La inversión refleja la carrera por infraestructura computacional necesaria para entrenar y servir modelos IA.
bajada_en: Google announced a $1.5 billion investment for 2026-2027 to expand its data center campus in Jackson County, Alabama. The investment reflects the race for computational infrastructure needed to train and serve AI models.

title: Meta Tapped a Pentagon Supplier to Prototype Face Recognition for Its Glasses
summary: Rank One, whose board includes a former CIA deputy director and a former FBI science chief, supplied face recognition to Meta for internal development of its smart glasses app.
title_es: Meta colaboró con proveedora del Pentágono en reconocimiento facial
bajada: Meta trabajó con Rank One Computing, cuya junta directiva incluye un exdirector de la CIA y un exjefe científico del FBI, para desarrollar reconocimiento facial para gafas inteligentes. La colaboración plantea preguntas sobre la relación entre tecnología de vigilancia comercial y gubernamental.
bajada_en: Meta worked with Rank One Computing, whose board includes a former CIA deputy director and former FBI science chief, to develop face recognition for smart glasses. The collaboration raises questions about the relationship between commercial and government surveillance technology.

title: AI language models have favorite names, and we mapped them [R]
summary: It turns out LLMs have strong priors over character names that are model-specific and version-specific. If you find Elena Vasquez and Marcus Chen together on a website, there's a good chance Claude generated it.We stumbled on this as a side finding while working on a model diffing method (CDD), and it grew into its own paper. The short version: these names travel as correlated ensembles, appear across dozens of websites as volcano experts, podcast hosts, thriller protagonists, and authors of 1000+ papers published in two months.Then we found a third name in the ensemble. The collage in the comments shows three different websites independently hallucinating the same trio with AI stock photo faces.Preprint:https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02184submitted by/u/CebulkaZapiekana[link][comments]
title_es: Estudio: modelos IA tienen nombres favoritos predecibles
bajada: Investigadores descubrieron que los LLMs tienen preferencias distintivas y predecibles por ciertos nombres que varían según el modelo y versión, causando que nombres específicos aparezcan recurrentemente en contenido generado. El hallazgo ayuda a identificar contenido generado por IA y revela comportamientos de sesgo en modelos.
bajada_en: Researchers found that LLMs have distinctive, predictable preferences for certain names that vary by model and version, causing specific names to appear recurrently in generated content. The finding helps identify AI-generated content and reveals bias behaviors in models.

title: Orchestra-o1: Omnimodal Agent Orchestration
summary:
arXiv:2606.13707v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The recent success of agent swarms has shifted the paradigm of large language model (LLM)-based agents from single-agent workflows to multi-agent systems, highlighting the importance of agent orchestration for task decomposition and collaboration. However, existing orchestration frameworks are limited to a narrow set of modalities and struggle to generalize to more complex settings where heterogeneous modalities coexist and interact. This limitation becomes particularly pronounced in omnimodal scenarios, where tasks require the unified understanding and coordination of diverse inputs such as text, image, audio, and video. In this work, we propose Orchestra-o1, an omnimodal agent orchestration framework designed to support efficient agent collaboration across multiple modalities. Orchestra-o1 introduces a unified orchestration mechanism that enables modality-aware task decomposition, online sub-agent specialization, and parallel sub-task execution. This scalable design allows agent systems to effectively tackle complex real-world tasks involving heterogeneous information sources, surpassing the second-best approach by 10.3% accuracy on the OmniGAIA benchmark. Furthermore, we introduce decision-aligned group relative policy optimization (DA-GRPO), an efficient agentic reinforcement learning approach for training Orchestra-o1-8B, which also achieves state-of-the-art performance against all existing open-source omnimodal agents.
title_es: Orchestra-o1: framework para orquestación de agentes multimodales
bajada: Investigadores presentaron Orchestra-o1, un framework que permite coordinación eficiente de agentes IA multimodales para tareas complejas con texto, imagen, audio y video. El trabajo avanza el estado del arte en sistemas multi-agente al resolver descomposición de tareas heterogéneas.
bajada_en: Researchers presented Orchestra-o1, a framework enabling efficient coordination of multimodal AI agents for complex tasks involving text, image, audio, and video. The work advances multi-agent systems by solving heterogeneous task decomposition.

title: Hybrid Open-Ended Tri-Evolution Makes Better Deep Researcher
summary:
arXiv:2606.13710v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Deep research and agent evolution serve as de-facto tasks for AI agents in real-world applications toward artificial general intelligence. The former enables autonomous retrieval and integration of information in open-ended environments to tackle open-ended research tasks, yet it is constrained by the static parametric deep research capabilities of agent systems. The latter allows agents to autonomously interact with the environment to gain experiences that evolve model capabilities. However, its effectiveness has been widely validated only on verifiable tasks with standard answers, leaving a gap with open-ended research tasks. To bridge these two critical tasks, we propose the Hybrid Open-Ended Tri-Evolution (HOTE) framework, which leverages hybrid-mode reinforcement learning to facilitate the collaborative evolution of a proposer, solver and judge based on web-scale knowledge, moving toward autonomous evolving agents in open-ended tasks and environments. Extensive experiments on three long-form deep research benchmarks demonstrate that the 8B model trained via HOTE surpasses the strongest static open 8-32B models as well as those trained by state-of-the-art deep research training methods with less time overhead, and further verify that the evolution of all three modules in HOTE is indispensable.
title_es: HOTE: framework para evolución autónoma de agentes de investigación
bajada: Se propuso HOTE, un framework que usa aprendizaje por refuerzo híbrido para evolucionar autónomamente agentes que realizan investigación profunda en tareas abiertas. El enfoque busca cerrar la brecha entre agentes estáticos y capacidades adaptativas para investigación real.
bajada_en: HOTE, a framework using hybrid reinforcement learning, was proposed to autonomously evolve agents performing deep research in open-ended tasks. The approach aims to close the gap between static agents and adaptive capabilities for real-world research.

title: Benchmarking Web Agent Safety under E-commerce Deceptive Interfaces
summary:
arXiv:2606.13686v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: As autonomous web agents are increasingly deployed to perform real-world tasks, ensuring their safety has become a critical concern. In this work, we study web agent behavior under realistic deceptive interfaces in the e-commerce domain. We introduce WebDecept, a lightweight and configurable plugin framework that enables controlled injection of deceptive interface patterns into existing web environments. Using WebDecept, we instantiate seven deceptive patterns commonly observed on the open web, including targeted advertisements, domain redirection, and shopping manipulation. By injecting these patterns into the frontend during task execution, we perform controlled evaluation of multiple multimodal web agents. Our results show that current web agents are highly susceptible to multiple classes of deceptive interfaces, and that prompt-based constraints are often insufficient to mitigate these failures. We further analyze how the design choices of deceptive patterns influence the success of such manipulations. These findings highlight safety challenges that should be addressed as web agents are scaled toward real-world deployment.
title_es: Estudio: agentes web vulnerables a interfaces engañosas
bajada: Investigadores introdujeron WebDecept, un framework para probar agentes web autónomos bajo interfaces engañosas e-commerce reales. El estudio encontró que agentes actuales son altamente susceptibles a manipulaciones y que restricciones por prompt son insuficientes.
bajada_en: Researchers introduced WebDecept, a framework to test autonomous web agents under realistic e-commerce deceptive interfaces. The study found current agents are highly susceptible to manipulations and prompt-based constraints are insufficient.
